name: Monte Busbeirón – circular from Santalla
name_original: Monte Busbeirón – circular desde Santalla – Montes de Fonsagrada
description: Hike the difficult 12.3-mile Monte Busbeirón circular from Santalla, climbing 2911 feet to the highest peak in Montes de Fonsagrada.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Negueira are set within a region characterized by a continuous alternation of mountains and deep valleys. The landscape features lush oak groves, pine woods, and riverine environments, including the Navia and Agüeira Rivers. Hikers can experience diverse terrain, from forested paths to areas offering panoramic views of the Sierra de Liñares and the Oscos area. Best family friendly hiking trails in Negueira The most popular family friendly hiking route is Chapel of Our Lady of Mount Carmel – Sculpture of the Pilgrim loop from Grandas de Salime, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied scenery around Grandas de Salime. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gold Trail, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This trail allows exploration of the natural environment and historical elements, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Local hikers also love the Route of the environments of Grandas - circular from Grandas de Salime, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Hiking in Negueira is defined by mountainous terrain, deep river valleys, and lush forests, with natural attractions like the Grandas de Salime Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, including several easy family hikes. The routes in Negueira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Negueira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Negueira?
A: Negueira offers a selection of 7 family-friendly hiking trails, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging routes. This includes 2 easy trails and 4 moderate options, ensuring there's something suitable for various family preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in Negueira?
A: The family-friendly trails in Negueira traverse a diverse landscape. You can expect to encounter lush oak groves, pine woods, and riverine environments along the Navia and Agüeira rivers. Many routes offer a mix of mountainous terrain and deep valleys, providing varied and scenic views.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, Negueira features several circular routes perfect for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239670030/gold-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gold Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.4 km circular path that allows you to explore ancient Roman gold mines and traditional villages. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047545115/route-of-the-environments-of-grandas-circular-from-grandas-de-salime" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the environments of Grandas&lt;/a&gt;, a longer moderate circular route.

Q: Can we bring our dog on these family-friendly hikes?
A: Negueira is generally welcoming to dogs on its hiking trails, making it a great destination for families with pets. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to carry waste bags to maintain the natural beauty of the region.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or special natural features to see on the trails?
A: Absolutely! Negueira is known for its beautiful natural features. You might discover magical waterfalls, such as the Fervenza de Pozo Negro, which is a highlight along some routes like the Route of the Three Manors. The region's rivers, like the Navia and Agüeira, also create picturesque riverine environments.

Q: What are the best trails for families with younger children?
A: For families with younger children, the easier trails are ideal. While specific easy routes are not detailed here, look for paths with shorter distances and minimal elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239670030/gold-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gold Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 7.4 km and moderate difficulty, could be a good option for slightly older children or those used to walking, offering interesting historical points.

Q: Where can we find parking for the family hiking trails?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting in or near villages like Grandas de Salime or Riodeporcos, you can often find designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details or local signage upon arrival for the most convenient parking spots.

Q: What kind of views can we expect from the family-friendly trails?
A: Negueira's trails offer stunning panoramic views. From various viewpoints, you can gain privileged perspectives of the Sierra de Liñares with the Navia River winding through the valley. Other routes provide vistas of the Oscos area and the majestic Sierra de los Ancares, showcasing the region's breathtaking natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the family-friendly trails in Negueira?
A: The family-friendly trails in Negueira are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars. Reviewers often praise the region's well-preserved natural landscapes, the variety of trails, and the peaceful atmosphere, making it a wonderful destination for outdoor family adventures.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the routes?
A: Yes, Negueira's trails often weave through areas rich in history and culture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239670030/gold-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gold Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past ancient Roman gold mines. You might also discover ethnographically valuable buildings like traditional hórreos (granaries) and paneras in villages along some routes, offering a glimpse into local heritage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking with family in Negueira?
A: The best time for family hiking in Negueira is generally during the spring and autumn months. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, vibrant foliage, and fewer crowds, making for a more comfortable and enjoyable experience. Summer can also be lovely, but it's wise to start earlier in the day to avoid the midday heat.
tips: Highest point of the Montes de Fonsagrada, with 1286 meters of altitude. Mount Busbeirón is located right on the border between Galicia and Asturias.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Monte Busbeirón circular hike?
faqs.answer: The most common starting point for this circular route is the village of Santalla. You should be able to find parking within the village, close to the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this is a challenging hike considered 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness.' The terrain can be rough and stony, and some sections may even demand alpine experience. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Busbeirón trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally permitted on trails in this region, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, difficult hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to tackle this hike is typically during late spring, summer, and early autumn (May to October). During these months, you'll generally find more favorable weather conditions, less snow at higher altitudes, and longer daylight hours. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: The Monte Busbeirón trail is located within the Río Eo, Oscos y Terras de Buron Biosphere Reserve. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking this route. However, always practice Leave No Trace principles and respect local regulations.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on the Monte Busbeirón circular?
faqs.answer: Expect a demanding hike with varied terrain. While there are 'mostly accessible paths,' you should be prepared for 'rough and stony ground' in many sections. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight of this route is reaching the summit of Monte Busbeirón, which is the highest point in the Montes de Fonsagrada. From here, you'll be rewarded with picturesque mountain scenery across the Cantabrian Mountains, spanning the border between Galicia and Asturias.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on Monte Busbeirón?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, pack essentials like plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a first-aid kit, navigation tools (map, compass, or GPS), and appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather. Sturdy hiking boots are a must, and trekking poles can be very helpful on the steep and uneven sections.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Santalla?
faqs.answer: Santalla is a small village, so amenities might be limited. It's advisable to bring your own food and drinks for the hike. You might find a local bar or small shop, but it's best to check opening hours in advance or plan to visit larger towns nearby for refreshments after your hike.

faqs.question: Is there an insider tip for tackling this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation gain and distance, starting your hike early in the morning is highly recommended. This allows ample time to complete the route before sunset and avoids the hottest part of the day, especially during summer months. Also, be sure to carry enough water, as sources on the trail may be scarce.

faqs.question: Are there any wild swimming spots near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the Montes de Fonsagrada region is known for its natural beauty, specific wild swimming spots directly adjacent to the Monte Busbeirón trail are not commonly highlighted. The focus of this particular route is the mountain summit. For swimming, you might need to explore other areas or rivers within the broader Río Eo, Oscos y Terras de Buron Biosphere Reserve.
